description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, cylindrical handle and two large, symmetrical black blades. The blades are wide, crescent-shaped, and feature a sharp, silver edge, with a central metallic mounting that appears reinforced and slightly ornate. The handle is black, segmented with raised rings for grip, and ends in a rounded pommel. The central mounting of the blades is metallic and features decorative elements, possibly rivets or studs, adding to its visual complexity. The axe has a menacing, medieval-inspired design, suitable for display or ceremonial use, but also appears functional. The overall construction suggests durability and strength, with a focus on both aesthetics and utility.
description: A double-headed black battle axe with wide crescent blades and a segmented, grippy black handle. The central mounting is metallic and ornate, giving the axe a striking and robust appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical black blades and the ornate central mounting.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, displaying the back of the blades and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and handle segmentation.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical black handle with segmented grip rings, likely wood wrapped in leather for enhanced grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, wide crescent-shaped blades with sharp silver edges and a central ornate metallic mounting; symmetrical and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and riveted mounting with visible metallic reinforcement at the blade-handle junction.
